channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Impact of Race in Canadian Sentencing: A Critique
item.description: The National Post editorial critiques the growing trend of race-based sentencing in Canadian courts, arguing that it undermines the principle of justice being blind. The article highlights a case wher...

item.title: Mithika Linturi Accuses Leaders of Neglecting Meru Banditry Victims, Plans Legal Action and Peaceful Protests
item.description: Former Meru Senator Mithika Linturi, together with other leaders from the Democracy for Citizens Party (DCP), has faulted elected officials in Meru County for staying silent as communities continue su...

item.title: Semmalai urges political parties to prioritize development over freebies
item.description: Former AIADMK Minister S. Semmalai has criticized the practice of political parties offering excessive freebies as part of their electoral promises. Speaking on March 21, 2026, Semmalai emphasized tha...

item.title: President Mwinyi urges peace, unity, and continued good deeds after Ramadhan
item.description: Zanzibar President Dr Hussein Ali Mwinyi has urged Tanzanians to uphold unity, peace, and the spirit of good deeds cultivated during the Holy Month of Ramadhan. Speaking during the national Eid Baraza...

item.title: Phone Technician Allegedly Killed by Police Over KSh 2,000 in Tanzania, Family Calls for Justice
item.description: Rashid Masunga, a 31-year-old phone technician from Mwanza, Tanzania, tragically lost his life after being allegedly murdered by a police officer. The incident occurred at night when Masunga was repor...

item.title: South Africa Maintains Stable Fuel Supply Amidst Global Tensions
item.description: The Department of Mineral and Petroleum Resources (DMPR) in South Africa has reassured citizens that the country's fuel supply remains stable in the short term, despite ongoing volatility in global en...

item.title: BTS return to the stage in Seoul with renewed energy and unity
item.description: BTS have made a powerful return to the stage with a live concert streamed worldwide from Gwanghwamun Square in Seoul, attracting an audience of 100,000 fans and millions more via Netflix. It marked th...

item.title: Key Considerations for Moving Your Super to an SMSF in Your 50s
item.description: A couple in their mid-50s is weighing up whether to transfer their superannuation into a self-managed super fund (SMSF) or continue with a retail fund or wrap platform. They understand the general pro...

item.title: Abuja Businessman Alleges SIU Officers Collected N5 Million for Bail
item.description: For Abuja, one businessman wey dem call Desmond Ediagbonya don accuse some police officers from the Special Investigation Unit (SIU) of collecting N5 million from am before dem release am on bail. Acc...

item.title: Northern Lights Forecast to Glow Again Across the UK and Beyond
item.description: Parts of the United Kingdom were treated to stunning views of the Northern Lights on Friday night, with vibrant displays also seen in other parts of the world such as Estonia, Canada, and Russia. The ...

item.title: IFP Reclaims Nongoma Majority in Recent By-Elections Victory
item.description: The Inkatha Freedom Party (IFP) has triumphed in three of the five wards contested in the March 18, 2026 by-elections. A key victory saw the IFP reclaim Ward 7 and Ward 20 in Nongoma Local Municipalit...

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs strengthen CAF hopes with solid win against Magesi FC
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs boosted their hopes of qualifying for continental football next season with a convincing 2-0 win over Magesi FC in a Betway Premiership clash at Moses Mabhida Stadium. The victory marked...

item.title: ReactOS Achieves Major Compatibility with Intel, NVIDIA, and AMD GPU Drivers
item.description: ReactOS, an open-source operating system that aims to achieve compatibility with Microsoft Windows applications and drivers, has announced a significant milestone in hardware support. The project’s de...

item.title: Iran's Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant Struck in Attack, Radiation Situation Normal
item.description: A projectile struck the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant in Iran on March 18, 2026, sparking concerns about a potential nuclear disaster. The plant, operated by Russia's Rosatom with Russian-made uranium, ...

item.title: MUHAS partners with Oxford to boost cancer research and care in Tanzania
item.description: MUHIMBILI University of Health and Allied Sciences (MUHAS) has entered into a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the University of Oxford to advance cancer care research in Tanzania. This collabor...

item.title: Casualties reported as Russia and Ukraine continue attacks ahead of U.S.-Ukraine discussions
item.description: At least four people were killed on Saturday as Russia and Ukraine exchanged attacks ahead of anticipated discussions between the U.S. and Ukraine on ending the conflict. In the Ukrainian city of Zapo...

item.title: 75-year-old grandmother loses £600k home after neighbour dispute over parking
item.description: A 75-year-old church-going grandmother, Marie Potter, has been left bankrupt after a prolonged legal battle with her neighbour, Kirsten McGowan, over a parking dispute in South London. The feud, which...

item.title: Former Brewdog Employees Criticise Rehiring Process After Tilray Takeover
item.description: Brewdog has faced criticism from trade union Unite following accusations that the company has used a 'fire and rehire' approach with former employees of its Glasgow Merchant City bar. The controversy ...

item.title: Authorities seize P20 million illegal cigarettes in Maguindanao checkpoint
item.description: In Parang, Maguindanao del Norte, law enforcement authorities successfully seized smuggled cigarettes valued at P20 million. The operation was a joint effort involving the Regional Mobile Force Battal...

item.title: Moxie Marlinspike's Confer Platform to Encrypt Meta AI Conversations
item.description: Moxie Marlinspike, the founder of Signal, is working on integrating his privacy-focused AI platform, Confer, into Meta's AI systems to protect user data in AI conversations. Marlinspike has a strong t...
